title: how to deal with Eva fascination
display_title: how to deal with Eva fascination
wikitext:
[]: how to deal with Eva fascination.             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Thu Nov 15, 2007 2:23 pm how do you guys manage your interest in Eva with your other priorities in life?

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Thu Nov 15, 2007 2:25 pm The back button on my internet browser. Eva's nothing more than a minor interest for me nowadays. And it's never gotten to the point where I had to consciously think about prioritizing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 15, 2007 2:55 pm With me it's not much of a problem. Most activities I'm involved with (my work, especially) allows me to focus on Eva in my downtime. The rest of the time if I'm doing something else I'm simply doing something else and usually not thinking about it.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Nov 15, 2007 3:10 pm Im in a place much like Evayojimbo, I'm really busy now a days

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Nov 15, 2007 4:21 pm Evangelion takes a back seat to most other things in my life. I really only devote time to it when I have it free.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Nov 15, 2007 5:11 pm Whenever your interest become too heatedly intense, you can always just rub one out. That might help.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Nov 15, 2007 6:05 pm @ Brik: LOL! Heh, part of the problem with me is Eva DOES become a higher priority than college (not good). What helps with me though is: 1) Hitting the back button on my browser if I attempt to pop in 2) Working on schoolwork first so I can geek out later.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Thu Nov 15, 2007 6:51 pm I simply prioritize. Eva's great and all, but it's not going to get me a better job. ^^

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Nov 15, 2007 8:52 pm BrikHaus wrote: Whenever your interest become too heatedly intense, you can always just rub one out. That might help.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Nov 16, 2007 1:50 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: @ Brik: LOL! Heh, part of the problem with me is Eva DOES become a higher priority than college (not good). What helps with me though is: 1) Hitting the back button on my browser if I attempt to pop in 2) Working on schoolwork first so I can geek out later.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Nov 16, 2007 2:23 am idk my school work keeps me distracted for the most part. and when im hanging out with friends. but whenever i have free time to myself i either contemplate plotholes in Evangelion or rub one out like Brik said. lol jk (i only rub one out)

[]: Re: how to deal with Eva fascination.             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Nov 16, 2007 4:40 am bap_120790 wrote: how do you guys manage your interest in Eva with your other priorities in life?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 4:29 pm I am obsessed, but I don't priotize. Priority list: 1) Homework 2) Trying to become a Regimental Sergeant Marjor (WO1) in the Cadets 3) Music (heck, I have to play bloody concertos) 4) Evading stupid comments from Retardus Maximus, Retardus Secondus et al. 5) Being introverted as much as possible 6) Wagnerian passions! =6) Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Sun Nov 18, 2007 4:53 pm I'd like to delude myself into thinking homework has a higher priority, but since I'm writing an Eva lemon ad watching football when I should be doing a five page paper I'd say that's not really true.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 4:56 pm Hey, I am posting all of this while I ma desperately doing my homework! (andlistening to Wagner at the same time)

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:12 pm It's currently more of a minor interest. Though Rebuild might... relight that flame.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:16 pm I will probably remain a zealot for the rest of my life, even though I have winkles all over.
